_comment: MEASURED asset dimensions — the drop-in source of truth for collider baking. This is the output slot for the bounding-box normalization build: it must contain, per catalog type (keys match props.json / fixtures.json entry names), the item's NORMALIZED WORLD-SPACE bounding box as actually placed in the map — { w, h, d } in world units (the same units maps.json positions use). NOT the GLB's raw native bbox: store the final rendered/placed size so physics and rendering consume it directly with no scale inference. When a type has an entry here, shared/physics.js bakes its collider as a cuboid from these exact bounds (per the design: 'cuboid from the measured bounds; trimesh only where a cuboid is clearly wrong'), and js/scene.js scales the GLB mesh to the same box so collider and mesh stay in lockstep. Types with NO entry here fall back to the hand-authored primitive footprint in props.json/fixtures.json (w/h/d/r) — never a hard failure. SHIPPED EMPTY on purpose: until the bounding-box build populates `dims`, behavior is byte-for-byte the pre-seam build (zero regression). See memory/notes/asset-dims.md for the wiring and memory/notes/physics.md for how colliders consume it.
